user_message: Give me the renewal risk for my team. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 15:55 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Fourth Coffee renewal risk

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-02-28 09:07 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Fourth Coffee renewal risk

There's been a change in our timeline for renewal risk. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-15 14:28 EST
Subject: Follow-up: renewal risk Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: renewal risk Discussion - Fourth Coffee
Date: 2024-01-06
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: David Rivera (Fourth Coffee), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), Priya (Fourth Coffee)

[00:01:30] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into renewal risk.

[00:08:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:10:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:13:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] Priya (Fourth Coffee): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:23:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:27:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-F trap (Omits likely objections section); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-F failure mode (Omits likely objections section)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-F -- Omits likely objections section
